pairs.en: All right, Thank you. One moment for our next question. And our next question will come from the line of Karl Keirstead from UBS. Your line is open.
pairs.zh:
好的，谢谢。请稍等，下一个问题。下一个问题来自UBS的Karl Keirstead。请提问。

All right, Thank you. One moment for our next question. And our next question will come from the line of Karl Keirstead from UBS. Your line is open.

pairs.en: Karl Keirstead--UBS -- Analyst
pairs.zh:
Karl Keirstead——瑞银集团——分析师

Karl Keirstead--UBS -- Analyst

pairs.en: Well, thank you. Maybe I'll direct these to Dev. Dev, I know it's hard to get into the heads of your customers and understand their behavior, but I guess the spirit of this question is when you say macro, what do you mean exactly? What are the customers telling you is the root cause? Is it a sensitivity to rates or consumer end-market weakness? Are you able to pinpoint what it is? And then, maybe part two, one easy alternative explanation to macro is that AI has become such a big issue for CIOs and boardrooms that it might be crowding out other spend. Do you feel like there's any credence to that thesis? Thank you for both, Dev.
pairs.zh:
好的，谢谢。也许我把问题转给Dev。Dev，我知道要深入了解客户的想法并理解他们的行为并不容易，但我想这个问题的关键在于，你说“宏观”时，具体指的是什么？客户告诉你的根本原因是什么？是对利率的敏感性，还是消费终端市场的疲软？你能明确指出是什么吗？然后，也许第二部分，对宏观因素的一个简单替代解释是，AI对CIOs和董事会来说已经成为一个如此重大的议题，以至于它可能正在挤出其他支出。你觉得这个说法有可信度吗？Dev，两个问题都谢谢你。

Well, thank you. Maybe I'll direct these to Dev. Dev, I know it's hard to get into the heads of your customers and understand their behavior, but I guess the spirit of this question is when you say macro, what do you mean exactly? What are the customers telling you is the root cause? Is it a sensitivity to rates or consumer end-market weakness? Are you able to pinpoint what it is? And then, maybe part two, one easy alternative explanation to macro is that AI has become such a big issue for CIOs and boardrooms that it might be crowding out other spend. Do you feel like there's any credence to that thesis? Thank you for both, Dev.

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. So thanks for the question, Karl. So what we talk about in macro, remember, ultimately we're a database or a data platform and the usage of our platform is directly or very tightly correlated to the performance of the end-customer's business. If they're selling 100 widgets a week and all of a sudden now they're selling 80 widgets a week, that will mean that they're using the database less intensely.
pairs.zh:
是的，感谢你的提问，Karl。我们在宏观层面讨论的是，记住，我们本质上是一个数据库或数据平台，而平台的使用与终端客户业务的表现直接或高度相关。如果他们每周卖100个Widget，突然降到每周80个，那就意味着他们对数据库的使用强度会下降。

原文：
Yeah. So thanks for the question, Karl. So what we talk about in macro, remember, ultimately we're a database or a data platform and the usage of our platform is directly or very tightly correlated to the performance of the end-customer's business. If they're selling 100 widgets a week and all of a sudden now they're selling 80 widgets a week, that will mean that they're using the database less intensely.

pairs.en: So when we see broad-based slowdown across different customer cohorts of different sizes, across different industries and across different geos, that strikes us as pretty much a macro issue. And so that's why, based on -- and we have close to 50,000 customers, so we have a pretty good feel for what's happening right now. And that's why we feel that there's definitely a macro element to it. With regards to the second part of your question is AI essentially crowding out new business.
pairs.zh:
因此，当我们看到不同规模、不同行业、不同地区的客户群体普遍出现放缓时，这让我们认为这基本上是一个宏观问题。正因如此，基于我们拥有近50,000家客户，我们对当前形势有相当清晰的感知。这也是我们认为其中肯定存在宏观因素的原因。至于你问题的第二部分：人工智能是否正在挤占新业务。

So when we see broad-based slowdown across different customer cohorts of different sizes, across different industries and across different geos, that strikes us as pretty much a macro issue. And so that's why, based on -- and we have close to 50,000 customers, so we have a pretty good feel for what's happening right now. And that's why we feel that there's definitely a macro element to it. With regards to the second part of your question is AI essentially crowding out new business.

pairs.en: We definitely think that that's plausible. We definitely see development teams experimenting on AI projects. The technology's changing very, very quickly. But that being said, we don't see that as a reason for us to not hit our new business targets.
pairs.zh:
我们确实认为这是可行的。我们确实看到开发团队在AI项目上进行尝试。技术正在非常非常快速地变化。但话虽如此，我们不认为这意味着我们不能实现我们的新业务目标。

We definitely think that that's plausible. We definitely see development teams experimenting on AI projects. The technology's changing very, very quickly. But that being said, we don't see that as a reason for us to not hit our new business targets.

pairs.en: And as I said, even though we started slow, we almost caught up at the end of this quarter, and we feel really good about our new business opportunity for the rest of this year. So I don't want to use that as an excuse for us not meeting our new business targets.
pairs.zh:
正如我所说，尽管我们起步较慢，但本季度末我们几乎赶上了进度，而且我们对今年剩余时间的新业务机会感到非常乐观。所以我不想把这作为我们未能达到新业务目标的借口。

And as I said, even though we started slow, we almost caught up at the end of this quarter, and we feel really good about our new business opportunity for the rest of this year. So I don't want to use that as an excuse for us not meeting our new business targets.

pairs.en: Kash Rangan--Goldman Sachs -- Analyst
pairs.zh:
Kash Rangan--高盛--分析师  
Kash Rangan--Goldman Sachs -- Analyst

pairs.en: OK. Got it. Helpful, thank you.
pairs.zh:
好的。明白了。很有帮助，谢谢。

OK. Got it. Helpful, thank you.

pairs.en: Operator
pairs.zh:
操作员
Operator

pairs.en: Thank you. One moment for our next question. Our next question will come from a line of Brad Sills from Bank of America. Your line is open.
pairs.zh:

Thank you. One moment for our next question. Our next question will come from a line of Brad Sills from Bank of America. Your line is open.

Yeah. The operational stuff and the slow start at the beginning that we kicked off is just trying to put the Q1 lighter new business quarter into context and is a nuance that's meant to help understand. It's not some fundamental different shift about the macroeconomic environment. We've been able to continue to execute well from that standpoint from go-to-market and everything else perspective. So just to try and connect the dots for those who are listening and have kind of seen the longitudinal story here.

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. I mean, it really depends on the -- if it's a new workload or existing workload. So if it's a new workload, it's hard to suss out exactly how quickly that workload is going to grow. But what we've changed is in our incentive mechanisms that there's more balance to quality in terms of size versus just pure volume.
pairs.zh:
是的，我的意思是，这实际上取决于它是新工作负载还是现有工作负载。如果是新工作负载，很难准确判断它将增长多快。但我们所做的改变是调整了激励机制，使其在规模与纯粹的数量之间更注重质量的平衡。

原文：
Yeah. I mean, it really depends on the -- if it's a new workload or existing workload. So if it's a new workload, it's hard to suss out exactly how quickly that workload is going to grow. But what we've changed is in our incentive mechanisms that there's more balance to quality in terms of size versus just pure volume.

pairs.en: Again, I just want to remind people we had record workload volumes last year, which so the intent of our strategy actually worked well because we really reduced the amount of friction in terms of acquiring new workloads. Just now that we're seeing, you know, starting to see one-year data, we just made fine-tuned some of our incentives to just make sure there's a little bit more balance on rewarding people for the size of workload, not just the volume.
pairs.zh:
再次提醒大家，去年我们创下了工作负载量纪录，因此我们的策略意图实际上效果很好，因为我们确实减少了获取新工作负载时的阻力。现在，随着我们看到第一年的数据，我们刚刚微调了一些激励措施，以确保在奖励员工时更注重大小而非单纯数量。

原文：
Again, I just want to remind people we had record workload volumes last year, which so the intent of our strategy actually worked well because we really reduced the amount of friction in terms of acquiring new workloads. Just now that we're seeing, you know, starting to see one-year data, we just made fine-tuned some of our incentives to just make sure there's a little bit more balance on rewarding people for the size of workload, not just the volume.

pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. So when we talk about consumption weakness, it is in our view the end applications usage slowing down which is in some ways a proxy for our end customers' businesses slowing down. And it's not new business because this is consumption of existing workloads. And you have to remember, we never saw really optimization as a major dynamic in our business last year.
pairs.zh:
是的。所以当我们谈论消费疲软时，我们认为这是终端应用使用放缓的表现，这在某种程度上反映了我们终端客户业务的放缓。这并不是新业务，因为这是对现有工作负载的消耗。而且你们要记住，去年我们从未真正看到优化成为我们业务中的主要动态。

原文：Yeah. So when we talk about consumption weakness, it is in our view the end applications usage slowing down which is in some ways a proxy for our end customers' businesses slowing down. And it's not new business because this is consumption of existing workloads. And you have to remember, we never saw really optimization as a major dynamic in our business last year.

pairs.en: So it wasn't a headwind last year, so it's not really a tailwind this year. And so that is not a major dynamic in our business relative to other consumption oriented businesses that obviously you track.
pairs.zh:
所以去年并不是逆风，因此今年也不算是顺风。相对于其他你显然关注的消费导向型企业而言，这并非我们业务中的主要动态因素。

So it wasn't a headwind last year, so it's not really a tailwind this year. And so that is not a major dynamic in our business relative to other consumption oriented businesses that obviously you track.

pairs.en: Yeah. And Tyler, I think we said this in the prepared remarks, but again, just to make sure that we hit this point. If you think about the slower growth in Atlas consumption that we saw, we talked about how, if you look on a year-over-year basis, the underlying usage, right, think like, you know, reads and writes, that grew more slowly this year as well. And as we've talked about, there tends to be a tight linkage between those two, given sort of the way that our value proposition works.
pairs.zh:

原文：Yeah. And Tyler, I think we said this in the prepared remarks, but again, just to make sure that we hit this point. If you think about the slower growth in Atlas consumption that we saw, we talked about how, if you look on a year-over-year basis, the underlying usage, right, think like, you know, reads and writes, that grew more slowly this year as well. And as we've talked about, there tends to be a tight linkage between those two, given sort of the way that our value proposition works.

Tyler Radke--Citi--分析师

pairs.en: OK. Helpful. I know you said that this year is obviously starting off slower than you expected and kind of behind seasonal trend versus last year. What are you doing from an adjustment perspective? Are you taking kind of a worst case scenario? Are you looking back, maybe a couple years back when consumption trends were even worse just help us understand what's embedded and what level of conservatism you're applying? Thank you.
pairs.zh:
好的，明白了。Michael，关于业绩指引，显然你也不喜欢下调指引，但既然这次已经下调了，希望这是今年最后一次。能否提醒我们，你们在消费模式上做了哪些假设？我知道你说今年开年比预期要慢，而且落后于去年的季节性趋势。从调整角度来看，你们做了什么？是采取最坏的情况吗？还是回顾了前几年消费趋势更糟糕的时候？请帮助我们理解你们目前假设的内容以及采用了多大的保守程度？谢谢。

OK. Helpful. I know you said that this year is obviously starting off slower than you expected and kind of behind seasonal trend versus last year. What are you doing from an adjustment perspective? Are you taking kind of a worst case scenario? Are you looking back, maybe a couple years back when consumption trends were even worse just help us understand what's embedded and what level of conservatism you're applying? Thank you.

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Yes, I'm happy to help you walk through. Obviously, we went through the fiscal 2025 guide in a fair amount of detail when we guided back in March, and so maybe I'll kind of call out the things that have changed since then in terms of our understanding, obviously first and foremost the Q1 results, being the biggest piece. So as we've talked about, consumption and consumption growth and consumption growth trends tend to be the biggest near-term factor when you look out at the business. So we saw those lower, as we mentioned, in March and April than what we expected.
pairs.zh:
好的，我很乐意帮你梳理一下。显然，在3月给出指引时，我们对2025财年指引进行了相当详细的说明，所以我可能会重点指出自那以来我们在理解上发生的变化——首先最重要的当然是第一季度业绩。正如我们讨论过的，消费、消费增长及消费增长趋势通常是观察业务时最关键的短期因素。因此，正如我们提到的，3月和4月的消费数据低于我们的预期。

原文：
Yes, I'm happy to help you walk through. Obviously, we went through the fiscal 2025 guide in a fair amount of detail when we guided back in March, and so maybe I'll kind of call out the things that have changed since then in terms of our understanding, obviously first and foremost the Q1 results, being the biggest piece. So as we've talked about, consumption and consumption growth and consumption growth trends tend to be the biggest near-term factor when you look out at the business. So we saw those lower, as we mentioned, in March and April than what we expected.

pairs.en: May, as I mentioned, is consistent with that, and so we've assumed that same level persists throughout the year. So we haven't assumed a recovery, and nor have we assumed a deterioration. I think the other thing within Atlas that's important to keep in mind, as you think through kind of the impacts, are in part because of that lower expansion in Q1 and then also the smaller new business cohort within Atlas in Q1. The starting Q2 ARR is lower, and that compounds over the course of the year, right? So we spent a bunch of time last year talking about how Q1 was a particularly strong quarter.
pairs.zh:
正如我提到的，五月的情况与此一致，因此我们假设这一水平将全年保持。我们既未假设复苏，也未假设恶化。我认为在分析影响时，还有一点需要记住：部分原因是第一季度扩张放缓，以及Atlas在第一季度的新业务群体规模较小。第二季度起步的ARR较低，并且这一情况会在全年持续累积，对吧？去年我们花了很多时间讨论第一季度是多么强劲的一个季度。

May, as I mentioned, is consistent with that, and so we've assumed that same level persists throughout the year. So we haven't assumed a recovery, and nor have we assumed a deterioration. I think the other thing within Atlas that's important to keep in mind, as you think through kind of the impacts, are in part because of that lower expansion in Q1 and then also the smaller new business cohort within Atlas in Q1. The starting Q2 ARR is lower, and that compounds over the course of the year, right? So we spent a bunch of time last year talking about how Q1 was a particularly strong quarter.

pairs.en: And given the math of compounding, that wound up being the gift that kept giving throughout the year from an absolute revenue number perspective, even if we saw different consumption trends, that will work the same way this year, but sort of in reverse, right? With the softer growth in Q1, that will compound, as you think about it, relative to your full year guide. And so that's baked in to the [Inaudible] equation. And the third thing that I call out is I referenced EA. EA did have a stronger Q1 and outperformed relative to our expectations.
pairs.zh:
考虑到复利的计算方式，从绝对收入数字的角度来看，这最终成为全年持续带来回报的礼物，即使我们看到了不同的消费趋势，今年也会以同样的方式运作，但某种程度上是反向的，对吧？随着第一季度增长放缓，当你考虑全年指引时，这会产生复利效应。因此，这已经融入[听不清]的公式中。我要提到的第三点是EA。EA确实在第一季度表现更强劲，超出了我们的预期。

And given the math of compounding, that wound up being the gift that kept giving throughout the year from an absolute revenue number perspective, even if we saw different consumption trends, that will work the same way this year, but sort of in reverse, right? With the softer growth in Q1, that will compound, as you think about it, relative to your full year guide. And so that's baked in to the [Inaudible] equation. And the third thing that I call out is I referenced EA. EA did have a stronger Q1 and outperformed relative to our expectations.

pairs.en: But despite that outperformance, we actually saw fewer multi-year deals and in the current macro environment, we look out, we think there's reason to believe that Q1 wasn't a fluke and that will be a headwind. And so, when you think about the 606 dynamics associated with enterprise advance, we factored that into account as well. And so, those are really the three key things that make up the inputs into the fiscal 2025 updated guide.
pairs.zh:
但尽管表现优异，我们实际看到的多年期交易反而减少了。在当前宏观环境下，展望未来，我们认为有理由相信第一季度并非偶然，而这将成为一项逆风因素。因此，在考虑与企业预付款相关的606动态时，我们也将其纳入了考量。以上就是构成2025财年更新指引输入的三个关键因素。

原文：But despite that outperformance, we actually saw fewer multi-year deals and in the current macro environment, we look out, we think there's reason to believe that Q1 wasn't a fluke and that will be a headwind. And so, when you think about the 606 dynamics associated with enterprise advance, we factored that into account as well. And so, those are really the three key things that make up the inputs into the fiscal 2025 updated guide.

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Potentially, I mean, I would say that we obviously have learned a lot. And so, we really indexed on volume because as in past years, when you have a portfolio of workloads, you're not sure which workload's going to take off. And that's been the growth driver of our business. I'll just remind you, five years ago, we were one-tenth of the size of our business today.
pairs.zh:
潜在上，我的意思是，我们显然学到了很多。因此，我们确实将重点放在了业务量上，因为过去几年里，当你拥有一个工作负载组合时，你无法确定哪个工作负载会增长起来。而这正是我们业务增长的驱动力。我只想提醒你，五年前，我们的业务规模只有现在的十分之一。

Potentially, I mean, I would say that we obviously have learned a lot. And so, we really indexed on volume because as in past years, when you have a portfolio of workloads, you're not sure which workload's going to take off. And that's been the growth driver of our business. I'll just remind you, five years ago, we were one-tenth of the size of our business today.

pairs.en: So that's, you know, our strategy has been to acquire workloads as quick as possible, and we want to make it even easier for customers to -- for us to win workloads, the customers could use our platform. It's just that as we see, the growth rates of the workloads that are more recently acquired, they seem to be growing a little slower than we expected. And so, we're just making some refinements. I don't want to suggest that this is some major pivot or kind of change in direction.
pairs.zh:
所以，我们的策略一直是尽快获取工作负载（workloads），我们希望让客户更容易——让我们更容易赢得工作负载，让客户能够使用我们的平台。只是正如我们所看到的，最近获取的工作负载的增长率似乎比我们预期的要慢一些。因此，我们只是在做一些优化调整。我不想暗示这是一个重大的转变或方向的改变。

原文：
So that's, you know, our strategy has been to acquire workloads as quick as possible, and we want to make it even easier for customers to -- for us to win workloads, the customers could use our platform. It's just that as we see, the growth rates of the workloads that are more recently acquired, they seem to be growing a little slower than we expected. And so, we're just making some refinements. I don't want to suggest that this is some major pivot or kind of change in direction.

pairs.en: It's just some refinements in terms of our incentive system to reward salespeople for workloads that grow even -- that grow fast. And so, and that's -- we think that's the appropriate response here.
pairs.zh:
这只是对我们激励制度的一些微调，用以奖励那些工作量增长——甚至快速增长——的销售人员。所以，我们认为这是恰当的应对方式。

It's just some refinements in terms of our incentive system to reward salespeople for workloads that grow even -- that grow fast. And so, and that's -- we think that's the appropriate response here.

pairs.en: Mike Cikos--Needham and Company -- Analyst
pairs.zh:
Mike Cikos--Needham and Company--分析师

Mike Cikos--Needham and Company -- Analyst

pairs.en: Got it. And I think my follow-up, again, I'm trying to get this from the outside in, but I'd appreciate any color. Like, understand the refinement here on looking at those workloads to grow faster, but I think Michael had made the earlier point as well that because of this go-to-market effort you almost by default have less visibility into that customer because you have reduced the friction to adopt. So can you help me think about how you guys are refining that focus on acquiring those right workloads, just given that reduced visibility we had?
pairs.zh:
明白了。我想追问一下，我尽量从外部视角理解，但希望能得到一些细节。比如，我理解你们在优化以更快增长的工作负载，但我觉得Michael之前也提到过，由于这种市场拓展策略，你几乎默认对客户的可见度更低，因为降低了采用门槛。那么，考虑到我们取得的可见度下降，能否帮我理清你们是如何优化对正确工作负载的获取的？

原文：
Got it. And I think my follow-up, again, I'm trying to get this from the outside in, but I'd appreciate any color. Like, understand the refinement here on looking at those workloads to grow faster, but I think Michael had made the earlier point as well that because of this go-to-market effort you almost by default have less visibility into that customer because you have reduced the friction to adopt. So can you help me think about how you guys are refining that focus on acquiring those right workloads, just given that reduced visibility we had?

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Well, I think it's really tied to the three priorities we outlined. So one, we're devoting more resources to the enterprise segment of the market. Well, we've seen great success there. We have lots of customers who spend eight figures with us.
pairs.zh:
嗯，我认为这确实与我们列出的三个优先事项相关。第一，我们将更多资源投入到企业市场领域。我们已经在这方面取得了巨大成功。很多客户在我们这里的消费达到了eight figures。

Well, I think it's really tied to the three priorities we outlined. So one, we're devoting more resources to the enterprise segment of the market. Well, we've seen great success there. We have lots of customers who spend eight figures with us.

pairs.en: We have lots of customers who spend seven figures with us. And we just see the returns on that segment be very, very strong. So we're obviously focused on that segment of the market. We talked about this focus on this kind of segment of the market that's been historically sort to hard to crack.
pairs.zh:
我们有许多客户在我们这里的消费达到seven figures。我们发现这一客户群体的回报非常、非常强劲。因此我们显然专注于这个市场细分领域。我们讨论过对这类市场细分领域的关注，这类市场历来有些难以攻克。

原文：We have lots of customers who spend seven figures with us. And we just see the returns on that segment be very, very strong. So we're obviously focused on that segment of the market. We talked about this focus on this kind of segment of the market that's been historically sort to hard to crack.

pairs.en: These legacy workloads run mission critical portions of these large businesses. But frankly, some of those developers have retired or left. And people are scared to make change because the time and the risk and the cost to do so can be quite difficult and risky. And so, essentially, by using AI, we've done some pilots, we can dramatically reduce the cost and time to make these migrations happen.
pairs.zh:
这些传统工作负载运行着这些大型企业的关键业务部分。但坦率地说，其中一些开发者已经退休或离开了。人们害怕进行更改，因为这样做的时间、风险和成本可能相当困难和危险。因此，本质上，通过使用AI，我们进行了一些试点，可以大幅降低实现这些迁移的成本和时间。

These legacy workloads run mission critical portions of these large businesses. But frankly, some of those developers have retired or left. And people are scared to make change because the time and the risk and the cost to do so can be quite difficult and risky. And so, essentially, by using AI, we've done some pilots, we can dramatically reduce the cost and time to make these migrations happen.

pairs.en: So that's generating a lot of interest and for customers, they're feeling a lot of pressure because the cost of bearing those legacy applications is very high. There's increasing regulatory and compliance pressures to upgrade those applications. Some of these technologies are end of life-ing, so they have a compelling reason to take action. And they want to also essentially position these apps to be AI enabled.
pairs.zh:
因此，这引起了广泛的关注，而客户们正感受到巨大压力，因为维护那些遗留应用的成本非常高。升级这些应用面临的监管和合规压力也在不断增加。其中一些技术正在逐步淘汰，因此他们有充分的理由采取行动。此外，他们本质上也希望这些应用能够具备AI能力。

So that's generating a lot of interest and for customers, they're feeling a lot of pressure because the cost of bearing those legacy applications is very high. There's increasing regulatory and compliance pressures to upgrade those applications. Some of these technologies are end of life-ing, so they have a compelling reason to take action. And they want to also essentially position these apps to be AI enabled.

pairs.en: And they can't do that with the legacy architectures that they have. So there's a whole set of reasons that these customers are interested in kind of migrating. And those workloads are definitely by definition bigger. And then, as I said, the third category is all about positioning ourselves for the coming set of AI apps that will come to production.
pairs.zh:
他们无法通过现有的传统架构做到这一点。因此，这些客户有诸多理由考虑迁移。而且，这些工作负载从定义上讲必然更大。此外，正如我所说，第三类则是为了让我们为即将投入生产的一系列AI应用做好准备。

原文：And they can't do that with the legacy architectures that they have. So there's a whole set of reasons that these customers are interested in kind of migrating. And those workloads are definitely by definition bigger. And then, as I said, the third category is all about positioning ourselves for the coming set of AI apps that will come to production.

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: But the other thing, Mike, just not to get lost in the details, but I wouldn't quite use the word visibility, but I do think if you think about it this way, if you're going through the process of negotiating a commitment with a customer, you're going to get an enormous amount of information from that customer about the workload and about everything else, and so when you're moving in a frictionless manner to get more workloads, you won't get all of that information. It's still possible with intent and purpose and incentives to get some of that information and getting that little bit of relevant information is dramatically different than getting a commitment, right? And so that's the balance that we're trying to strike and that we're continuing to iterate as we learn here.
pairs.zh:
另外，Mike，不要被细节迷惑——我不会用“可见性”这个词，但我确实认为，如果你这样想：当你与客户谈判承诺的过程中，你会从客户那里获得大量关于工作负载和其他所有信息。而当你以无摩擦的方式争取更多工作负载时，你无法获得所有这些信息。但通过意图、目的和激励，仍然有可能获得部分信息，而获取那一点点相关信息与获得承诺是截然不同的，对吧？这就是我们试图达成的平衡，并且在我们学习的过程中不断迭代。

原文：But the other thing, Mike, just not to get lost in the details, but I wouldn't quite use the word visibility, but I do think if you think about it this way, if you're going through the process of negotiating a commitment with a customer, you're going to get an enormous amount of information from that customer about the workload and about everything else, and so when you're moving in a frictionless manner to get more workloads, you won't get all of that information. It's still possible with intent and purpose and incentives to get some of that information and getting that little bit of relevant information is dramatically different than getting a commitment, right? And so that's the balance that we're trying to strike and that we're continuing to iterate as we learn here.

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. And I also just want to add, it's not that we're anti-commitments, but we want the customer to feel like, OK, I see enough volume either through this one workload or through the multitude of workloads they have to make a bigger commitment. And it's a much more natural conversation than trying to prematurely force a commitment when the customer themselves may not know how quickly that workload is going to grow. And so, consequently, we'll struggle to figure out what kind of commitment they want to sign up for.
pairs.zh:
是的，我还想补充一点，我们并不是反对承诺，而是希望客户能感觉到：“好吧，通过这个工作负载或多个工作负载，我看到足够的使用量，可以做出更大的承诺。” 这比在客户自己都不清楚工作负载增长有多快时就强行推动承诺要自然得多。因此，客户也很难搞清楚他们想要签署什么样的承诺。

原文：
Yeah. And I also just want to add, it's not that we're anti-commitments, but we want the customer to feel like, OK, I see enough volume either through this one workload or through the multitude of workloads they have to make a bigger commitment. And it's a much more natural conversation than trying to prematurely force a commitment when the customer themselves may not know how quickly that workload is going to grow. And so, consequently, we'll struggle to figure out what kind of commitment they want to sign up for.

pairs.en: Mike Cikos--Needham and Company -- Analyst
pairs.zh:
Mike Cikos--Needham and Company--分析师

Mike Cikos--Needham and Company -- Analyst

pairs.en: Got it. Thank you for polishing it up, Mike. I really appreciate all the detail. Thank you.
pairs.zh:
明白了。Mike，谢谢你帮忙润色。非常感谢你提供的所有细节。谢谢。

Got it. Thank you for polishing it up, Mike. I really appreciate all the detail. Thank you.

pairs.zh:
谢谢。[Operator instructions] 请稍等，等待下一个提问。下一位提问者来自Piper Sandler的Brent Bracelin。请开始提问。

Thank you. [Operator instructions] One moment for our next question. Our next question will come from Brent Bracelin from Piper Sandler. Your line is open.

pairs.en: Brent Bracelin--Piper Sandler -- Analyst
pairs.zh:
Brent Bracelin--Piper Sandler -- 分析师  
Brent Bracelin--Piper Sandler -- Analyst

pairs.en: Thank you. Good afternoon. Michael, wanted to go back to the slow start to new business. How much of that was internal versus a change in the external selling environment that created the slow start, be it lengthening sales cycles, longer close times, just trying to think through the internal or external factor there.
pairs.zh:
谢谢。下午好。Michael，我想回到新业务起步缓慢的问题上。造成这种缓慢起步的原因，有多少是内部因素，又有多少是外部销售环境的变化——比如销售周期延长、成交时间变长——只是试图理清内部或外部因素。

原文：
Thank you. Good afternoon. Michael, wanted to go back to the slow start to new business. How much of that was internal versus a change in the external selling environment that created the slow start, be it lengthening sales cycles, longer close times, just trying to think through the internal or external factor there.

pairs.en: Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer
pairs.zh:
迈克尔·戈登（Michael Gordon）——首席运营官兼首席财务官

Michael Gordon--Chief Operating Officer and Chief Financial Officer

pairs.en: Internal.
pairs.zh:
内部。
Internal.

pairs.en: Brent Bracelin--Piper Sandler -- Analyst
pairs.zh:
Brent Bracelin--Piper Sandler -- 分析师  
Brent Bracelin--Piper Sandler -- Analyst

pairs.en: OK. Very clear. That's helpful color. And then, Dev, for you, as we think about the business, what we're seeing a slowdown across the broader application software space, it does feel like there's a AI crowding out effect here temporarily, but as we think about the next year and what you can control to drive maybe an acceleration the business a year from now? What are the things you're focused on repositioning this company for improving growth next year?
pairs.zh:
好的，非常清楚。这个背景信息很有帮助。那么，Dev，关于业务，我们看到整个应用软件领域出现了放缓，感觉上这里暂时存在AI的挤出效应。但展望明年，以及你可以控制的部分，能否推动业务在一年后加速增长？你目前专注于哪些方面来重新定位公司，以在明年实现增长提升？

原文：
OK. Very clear. That's helpful color. And then, Dev, for you, as we think about the business, what we're seeing a slowdown across the broader application software space, it does feel like there's a AI crowding out effect here temporarily, but as we think about the next year and what you can control to drive maybe an acceleration the business a year from now? What are the things you're focused on repositioning this company for improving growth next year?

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. So you know, again, just to go back, I mean, we're going after a very large market. We have low share, but we have to acquire business workload by workload, which is harder than, say, getting a broad-based decision, the company to standardize the technology across the enterprise. The base is growing, but it is slowing over time.
pairs.zh:
是的。你知道，再次回顾一下，我的意思是，我们正在追求一个非常大的市场。我们的市场份额很低，但我们必须一个业务负载一个业务负载地去获取，这比获得一个广泛基础的决策——让公司在整个企业范围内标准化该技术——要困难得多。基础在增长，但随着时间的推移正在放缓。

Yeah. So you know, again, just to go back, I mean, we're going after a very large market. We have low share, but we have to acquire business workload by workload, which is harder than, say, getting a broad-based decision, the company to standardize the technology across the enterprise. The base is growing, but it is slowing over time.

pairs.en: And the key to us then is the pace of workload acquisition. So one thing that we're doing is, as I said earlier, is that we are growing our productive capacity and trying to do that consistently. We did pause last year, but we're investing this year. We're also trying to increase the productivity of that sales organization by getting better and more efficient at increasing the pace at which we can acquire workloads.
pairs.zh:
对我们来说，关键在于工作负载获取的速度。因此，我们正在做的一件事，正如我之前所说，就是扩大我们的生产能力，并努力持续这样做。去年我们确实暂停了，但今年我们在投资。我们还在努力提高销售组织的生产力，通过变得更优秀、更高效来加快我们获取工作负载的速度。

原文：
And the key to us then is the pace of workload acquisition. So one thing that we're doing is, as I said earlier, is that we are growing our productive capacity and trying to do that consistently. We did pause last year, but we're investing this year. We're also trying to increase the productivity of that sales organization by getting better and more efficient at increasing the pace at which we can acquire workloads.

pairs.en: Posting on the large segment or the large enterprise segment will help that. We also are trying to sell more top down. The whole focus on legacy app modernization is really elevating our conversations with senior level customers. Because they are in such pain right now that they want to talk to us and they're quite excited about the results that we've produced in some of these pilots.
pairs.zh:
在大客户或大企业领域发力会有所帮助。我们也在尝试更多地进行自上而下的销售。整个对legacy app modernization的关注确实提升了我们与高层客户的对话。因为他们目前正处于困境之中，所以想与我们交谈，而且他们对我们一些试点项目所取得的结果感到非常兴奋。

Posting on the large segment or the large enterprise segment will help that. We also are trying to sell more top down. The whole focus on legacy app modernization is really elevating our conversations with senior level customers. Because they are in such pain right now that they want to talk to us and they're quite excited about the results that we've produced in some of these pilots.

pairs.en: And we're talking to customers who are interested in really engaging with us in the program. And then, obviously, AI is also top of mind for senior level executives and we're using that as a way to also sell more top down. So those are the things that we're focused on, you know, in terms of how we continue to drive the long-term growth of the business. And as I said, we feel really good about the long-term prospects.
pairs.zh:
我们正在与那些有意积极参与我们项目的客户进行沟通。显然，AI也是高层管理人员首要考虑的事项，我们正利用这一点作为自上而下推动更多销售的方式。因此，这些就是我们在持续推动业务长期增长方面的关注重点。正如我所说，我们对长期前景感到非常乐观。

原文：
And we're talking to customers who are interested in really engaging with us in the program. And then, obviously, AI is also top of mind for senior level executives and we're using that as a way to also sell more top down. So those are the things that we're focused on, you know, in terms of how we continue to drive the long-term growth of the business. And as I said, we feel really good about the long-term prospects.

William Power——Robert W. Baird and Company——分析师

William Power--Robert W. Baird and Company -- Analyst

pairs.en: OK. Great. Thanks for taking the question. Maybe a slightly different tact.

好的。很好。感谢提出这个问题。也许换一种不同的策略。

OK. Great. Thanks for taking the question. Maybe a slightly different tact.

pairs.en: I guess, Dev, coming out of MongoDB Local in New York, you had a bunch of new product announcements. So it'd be great to kind of hear what customers were most excited about, where the conversations were focused, the meetings there. And I guess, secondly, I guess I'd be curious, just as you were talking to customers, kind of what their view was toward macro, versus investments because it sounds like a lot of the macro you're seeing on the consumption side and the underlying workloads, but I guess I'm curious kind of from a top-down level kind of what their views or conversations were around, macro versus investing in new technologies.
pairs.zh:
我想，Dev，在纽约的MongoDB Local活动结束后，你们发布了一系列新产品。所以如果能听听客户最兴奋的是什么，会议上的对话和会面主要聚焦在哪里，那就太好了。其次，我也很好奇，当你与客户交谈时，他们对宏观经济的看法与投资相比如何？因为你提到在消费端和底层工作负载上看到了很多宏观因素，但我好奇从自上而下的角度，他们对宏观经济与投资新技术之间的看法或讨论是怎样的。

"I guess, Dev, coming out of MongoDB Local in New York, you had a bunch of new product announcements. So it'd be great to kind of hear what customers were most excited about, where the conversations were focused, the meetings there. And I guess, secondly, I guess I'd be curious, just as you were talking to customers, kind of what their view was toward macro, versus investments because it sounds like a lot of the macro you're seeing on the consumption side and the underlying workloads, but I guess I'm curious kind of from a top-down level kind of what their views or conversations were around, macro versus investing in new technologies."

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah, so in terms of new products, obviously, the way we introduce new products is we first kind of do a beta rollout, get some early pilot customers, and then do more of a controlled introduction before you go to general availability. And so, for our stream processing product, we already had a number of customers, actually in the hundreds of customers who were testing and giving us feedback. The receptivity was very positive and the interest rate is very high because when you think about being able to process data in motion, most of that data is JSON based. It's obviously core to who we are as a platform and so it's well-suited as a kind of a something that should be part of the MongoDB platform.
pairs.zh:
嗯，关于新产品，显然我们的做法是先进行beta测试，获得一些早期试用客户，然后在正式发布前进行更受控的推广。对于我们的流处理产品，我们已经拥有数百名客户在测试并提供反馈。市场接受度非常积极，兴趣度也很高，因为当你考虑处理动态数据时，大部分数据都是基于JSON格式的。这显然是我们作为平台的核心能力，因此它非常适合作为MongoDB平台的一部分。

原文：
Yeah, so in terms of new products, obviously, the way we introduce new products is we first kind of do a beta rollout, get some early pilot customers, and then do more of a controlled introduction before you go to general availability. And so, for our stream processing product, we already had a number of customers, actually in the hundreds of customers who were testing and giving us feedback. The receptivity was very positive and the interest rate is very high because when you think about being able to process data in motion, most of that data is JSON based. It's obviously core to who we are as a platform and so it's well-suited as a kind of a something that should be part of the MongoDB platform.

pairs.en: And so that receptivity was quite high. Obviously 8.0, we've announced, but we have not really, it will be generally available later this year. But we have lots of customers, we have some of the most demanding and sophisticated customers who are pushing our platform. We by definition are much more performant and scalable than most of the platforms out there.
pairs.zh:
因此，客户的接受度相当高。显然，我们已经宣布了8.0版本，但尚未正式发布，它将在今年晚些时候全面上市。我们拥有大量客户，其中一些要求最苛刻、最专业的客户正在推动我们的平台发展。从定义上讲，我们平台在性能和可扩展性方面远超大多数现有平台。

And so that receptivity was quite high. Obviously 8.0, we've announced, but we have not really, it will be generally available later this year. But we have lots of customers, we have some of the most demanding and sophisticated customers who are pushing our platform. We by definition are much more performant and scalable than most of the platforms out there.

pairs.en: So by definition, we have customers who really push the envelope and they're really excited by the fact about the performance gains that we're delivering because that will only help them in terms of what they want to do. And then, in terms of the new products that we are in terms of full text search and vector search that we're introducing to the community. They're really excited because in many places for our prospects, that's where they start. They start with a community version before they say move to Atlas or move to EA.
pairs.zh:
因此，从定义上看，我们的客户确实在挑战极限，他们对我们所提供的性能提升感到非常兴奋，因为这只会帮助他们实现自己的目标。此外，就我们向社区推出的新产品——全文搜索和向量搜索而言，他们也非常兴奋，因为对于我们的潜在客户来说，这往往是他们起步的地方。他们会先使用社区版，然后才考虑迁移到Atlas或EA。

So by definition, we have customers who really push the envelope and they're really excited by the fact about the performance gains that we're delivering because that will only help them in terms of what they want to do. And then, in terms of the new products that we are in terms of full text search and vector search that we're introducing to the community. They're really excited because in many places for our prospects, that's where they start. They start with a community version before they say move to Atlas or move to EA.

pairs.en: And so, by basically allowing them to start using these new products when they're in the early prototyping and development phase of their project is actually in that goodness for them because then they can kind of start using all their products right from the gate versus having to start and then add on these other products later. So from that point of view, the sentiment and the positive is very high. With regards to the macro environment, I would say two things. One, at the high-end of the market, there's no question that customers are very cost conscious.
pairs.zh:
因此，基本上通过让他们在项目的早期原型和开发阶段就开始使用这些新产品，这实际上对他们是有利的，因为他们可以从一开始就使用所有产品，而不必先开始然后后期再添加这些其他产品。从这个角度来看，市场情绪非常积极。关于宏观环境，我想说两点。第一，在高端市场，毫无疑问客户对成本非常敏感。

And so, by basically allowing them to start using these new products when they're in the early prototyping and development phase of their project is actually in that goodness for them because then they can kind of start using all their products right from the gate versus having to start and then add on these other products later. So from that point of view, the sentiment and the positive is very high. With regards to the macro environment, I would say two things. One, at the high-end of the market, there's no question that customers are very cost conscious.

pairs.en: And so, I think that's playing out just because everyone's kind of watching their pennies. And so, we like the fact that we're very, on a price performance basis, a very attractive platform. But there's no question that customers are mindful about costs in this new kind of macroeconomic era. And then, on the lower end of the channel, it's also not a shock to say that obviously smaller customers are having more difficulty raising capital and consequently they have to be also more judicious about how they spend money and that also obviously factors in terms of how much they can spend on their own, internal technology stack.
pairs.zh:
因此，我认为这种情况正在发生，因为大家都在精打细算。我们很高兴自己是一个在性价比基础上非常有吸引力的平台。但毫无疑问，在这个新的宏观经济时代，客户对成本非常敏感。而在渠道的低端市场，毫不意外地说，较小型的客户在融资方面遇到更多困难，因此他们必须更加审慎地花钱，这显然也会影响他们能在自身内部技术栈上投入多少。

And so, I think that's playing out just because everyone's kind of watching their pennies. And so, we like the fact that we're very, on a price performance basis, a very attractive platform. But there's no question that customers are mindful about costs in this new kind of macroeconomic era. And then, on the lower end of the channel, it's also not a shock to say that obviously smaller customers are having more difficulty raising capital and consequently they have to be also more judicious about how they spend money and that also obviously factors in terms of how much they can spend on their own, internal technology stack.

Joe Vandrick--Scotiabank -- 分析师

Joe Vandrick--Scotiabank -- Analyst

pairs.en: Hi, this is Joe Vandrick on for Patrick Colville. So it seems like excitement on application modernization has kicked up a little bit, and it's now a key focus. So I'm curious, how are you defining and tracking success with the relational migrator product?
pairs.zh:
你好，我是Joe Vandrick，代替Patrick Colville。看来应用现代化的热度有所上升，现在成为了一个关键焦点。我很好奇，你们是如何使用relational migrator产品来定义和追踪成功的？

Hi, this is Joe Vandrick on for Patrick Colville. So it seems like excitement on application modernization has kicked up a little bit, and it's now a key focus. So I'm curious, how are you defining and tracking success with the relational migrator product?

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. So for those of you who may not know the full story, let me explain. So we have an existing relational migrator product that allows people to essentially migrate data from legacy relational databases and does the schema mapping for them. The one thing it does not do, which is the most cumbersome and tedious part of the migration, is to auto-generate or build application code.
pairs.zh:
是的。对于那些可能不了解整个情况的人，我来解释一下。我们现有的relational migrator产品让人们能够从传统关系型数据库迁移数据，并为他们完成模式映射。它唯独没有做到的是迁移中最繁琐、最乏味的部分，即自动生成或构建应用程序代码。

Yeah. So for those of you who may not know the full story, let me explain. So we have an existing relational migrator product that allows people to essentially migrate data from legacy relational databases and does the schema mapping for them. The one thing it does not do, which is the most cumbersome and tedious part of the migration, is to auto-generate or build application code.

pairs.en: So when you go from a relational app to an app built in MongoDB, you still have to essentially rewrite the application code. And for many customers, that was the inhibitor for them to migrate more apps because that takes a lot of time and a lot of labor resources. So our app monetization effort is all about, or using AI is all about now solving the third leg of that stool, which is being able to reduce the time and cost and effort of rewriting the app code, all the way from analyzing existing code, converting that code to new code, and then also building the test suites, both unit tests and functional tests, to be able to make sure the new app is obviously operating and functioning the way it should be. And so, those parts of the equation is what we are excited about because now AI can really help reduce the cost and complexity of rewriting app code.
pairs.zh:
因此，当你从关系型应用转向基于MongoDB构建的应用时，仍然需要从根本上重写应用程序代码。对许多客户而言，这正是阻碍他们迁移更多应用的因素，因为这会耗费大量时间和人力。因此，我们的应用变现策略，或者说借助AI的当前重点，正是解决这第三个关键环节——能够减少重写应用代码所需的时间、成本和精力，涵盖从分析现有代码、将其转换为新代码，再到构建测试套件（包括单元测试和功能测试）的全过程，以确保新应用能够按预期正常运行。这些环节正是我们所兴奋的，因为现在AI确实能够帮助降低重写应用代码的成本和复杂性。

So when you go from a relational app to an app built in MongoDB, you still have to essentially rewrite the application code. And for many customers, that was the inhibitor for them to migrate more apps because that takes a lot of time and a lot of labor resources. So our app monetization effort is all about, or using AI is all about now solving the third leg of that stool, which is being able to reduce the time and cost and effort of rewriting the app code, all the way from analyzing existing code, converting that code to new code, and then also building the test suites, both unit tests and functional tests, to be able to make sure the new app is obviously operating and functioning the way it should be. And so, those parts of the equation is what we are excited about because now AI can really help reduce the cost and complexity of rewriting app code.

pairs.en: And that's why customers are getting more excited, because the lower you reduce the cost for that migration or the switching cost, the more apps you can then, by definition, migrate. And so that is something that we are very excited about. I will caution you that it's early days. You should not expect some inflection in the business because of this.
pairs.zh:
正因如此，客户们变得更加兴奋，因为迁移成本或转换成本降得越低，理论上你能迁移的应用就越多。所以这正是我们非常兴奋的地方。但我要提醒大家，现在还处于早期阶段。你们不应期待这会给业务带来某种转折点。

And that's why customers are getting more excited, because the lower you reduce the cost for that migration or the switching cost, the more apps you can then, by definition, migrate. And so that is something that we are very excited about. I will caution you that it's early days. You should not expect some inflection in the business because of this.

pairs.en: But we're really excited about the opportunity, and we're also really excited about the fact that it gives us access to very senior decision makers because they by definition can make decisions quickly around what to do and what not to do.
pairs.zh:
但我们对此机会非常兴奋，也对它让我们接触到高级决策者这一事实感到振奋，因为他们顾名思义能够迅速决定哪些事该做、哪些不该做。

But we're really excited about the opportunity, and we're also really excited about the fact that it gives us access to very senior decision makers because they by definition can make decisions quickly around what to do and what not to do.

pairs.zh:
Joe Vandrick--Scotiabank -- 分析师

Joe Vandrick--Scotiabank -- Analyst

pairs.en: Got it. And one more if I could get it in total customer count continues to grow out of at a pretty decent pace and so does the customer account you know over a hundred thousand customers but it looks like the direct sales customer count growth has tapered-off a little bit. Could you talk through if that's just a function of going after more strategic customers or if it is maybe something else? Thanks.
pairs.zh:
明白。另外还有一个问题，如果可能的话，我想了解总客户数量持续以相当不错的速度增长，客户账户也是如此，目前超过十万客户，但看起来直接销售的客户数量增长有所放缓。您能否谈谈这是否只是因为我们在追求更具战略性的客户，还是可能有其他原因？谢谢。

Got it. And one more if I could get it in total customer count continues to grow out of at a pretty decent pace and so does the customer account you know over a hundred thousand customers but it looks like the direct sales customer count growth has tapered-off a little bit. Could you talk through if that's just a function of going after more strategic customers or if it is maybe something else? Thanks.

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Yeah. I mean, we're definitely interested in continuing to acquire customers. Obviously, we have a lot of our existing customers are still vastly under-penetrated in, So our biggest growth opportunities in the near-term are actually winning more workloads in our existing customer accounts. And as I mentioned, we have lots of eight figure customers and lots of seven figure customers along with the six figure customers.
pairs.zh:
是的，我们确实有兴趣继续获取客户。显然，我们现有的许多客户在渗透率上仍然远远不足，因此我们近期最大的增长机会实际上是在现有客户账户中赢得更多的工作负载。而且正如我提到的，我们有很多八位数客户、七位数客户以及六位数客户。

原文：
Yeah. I mean, we're definitely interested in continuing to acquire customers. Obviously, we have a lot of our existing customers are still vastly under-penetrated in, So our biggest growth opportunities in the near-term are actually winning more workloads in our existing customer accounts. And as I mentioned, we have lots of eight figure customers and lots of seven figure customers along with the six figure customers.

pairs.en: So we know that once you get in and we can kind of win a lot more business in those accounts. But we do have teams focused on acquiring new logos. That obviously is a longer sales cycle, but it's something that we do care about, especially at the higher end where there's still lots of enterprise logos where we are, they're not yet MongoDB customers, and we are definitely focused on making sure we crack into those accounts.
pairs.zh:
所以，我们知道一旦你进入这些客户，我们就能在这些账户中赢得更多业务。但同时，我们也有专门团队专注于获取新客户（new logos）。显然，这需要更长的销售周期，但这也是我们非常重视的事情，尤其是在高端市场，那里还有很多大型企业客户目前还不是MongoDB的客户，我们正全力确保成功打入这些客户。

原文：So we know that once you get in and we can kind of win a lot more business in those accounts. But we do have teams focused on acquiring new logos. That obviously is a longer sales cycle, but it's something that we do care about, especially at the higher end where there's still lots of enterprise logos where we are, they're not yet MongoDB customers, and we are definitely focused on making sure we crack into those accounts.

pairs.en: Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer
pairs.zh:
Dev Ittycheria——总裁兼首席执行官

Dev Ittycheria--President and Chief Executive Officer

pairs.en: Well, I want to thank everyone for joining us today. Just to kind of summarize the call, we are tempering our outlook for the rest of this year due to a more challenging macro environment. We are focusing more resources on the high end of the market, accelerating legacy app modernization using AI, and cementing our position as the platform of choice for next generation AI applications. And we'll invest judiciously in these priorities through the rest of this year.
pairs.zh:
好的，感谢各位今天参加我们的会议。简单总结一下本次电话会：鉴于宏观环境更具挑战性，我们正在调低对今年剩余时间的展望。我们将把更多资源聚焦于高端市场，利用AI加速传统应用的现代化改造，并巩固我们作为下一代AI应用首选平台的地位。今年余下时间，我们将对这些优先事项进行审慎投资。

Well, I want to thank everyone for joining us today. Just to kind of summarize the call, we are tempering our outlook for the rest of this year due to a more challenging macro environment. We are focusing more resources on the high end of the market, accelerating legacy app modernization using AI, and cementing our position as the platform of choice for next generation AI applications. And we'll invest judiciously in these priorities through the rest of this year.

pairs.en: We also remain confident in our foundational and durable technology advantage, which has been well suited as application development has evolved over time. And we believe this advantage will remain as customers build AI production apps at scale. Thank you for joining us and we'll talk to you soon. Take care.
pairs.zh:
我们也对我们的基础且持久的技术优势保持信心，这一优势随着应用开发的不断演进而一直非常契合。我们相信，当客户大规模构建AI生产应用程序时，这一优势将持续存在。感谢您的参与，我们很快再聊。保重。

We also remain confident in our foundational and durable technology advantage, which has been well suited as application development has evolved over time. And we believe this advantage will remain as customers build AI production apps at scale. Thank you for joining us and we'll talk to you soon. Take care.
